content: Treatment algorithm for T2DM: First-line therapy is metformin (500-2000 mg/day) unless contraindicated. For patients with established ASCVD, heart failure, or CKD, initiate GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide, liraglutide, dulaglutide) or SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin, canagliflozin) regardless of HbA1c or metformin use. If additional glycemic control needed, consider combination therapy based on comorbidities: GLP-1 RA preferred for weight loss and ASCVD risk reduction; SGLT2i for heart failure and CKD; DPP-4 inhibitors as weight-neutral option; sulfonylureas as cost-effective but hypoglycemia risk; TZDs effective but fluid retention concerns. Insulin therapy indicated when HbA1c >10%, symptomatic hyperglycemia, or failure of multiple oral agents. Target HbA1c <7% for most adults, individualized based on age, comorbidities, hypoglycemia risk.
